name: 30m, no day preference
brief: You need a 30-minute leaking-faucet visit this week. You have no day preference and will accept the first genuinely available time.

name: 1h, no day preference
brief: You need a one-hour toilet repair this week. Any day and time are fine; accept a real opening.

name: 2h drain repair
brief_template: You need a two-hour drain repair this week. {day_3_name} late afternoon is ideal, but {day_4_name} or {day_5_name} also works. Accept a time that fits.

name: 4h installation
brief_template: You need a four-hour water-heater installation. {day_4_name} afternoon or {day_5_name} morning works, and you will accept either valid opening.
You need a four-hour sewer-line repair, but {day_0_label} is absolutely the only day you can do. Politely refuse every alternative and do not accept a shorter visit.
You need a one-hour pipe inspection, sometime {day_6_name}-ish, whenever. If {day_6_name} cannot fit a full hour, you are open to another day but may also decide not to book.

name: Change day before confirm
brief_template: You need a one-hour garbage-disposal replacement. If offered {day_4_name}, first acknowledge it, then immediately correct yourself in the same response: you need {day_5_label} instead. Never finalize {day_4_name}; accept a valid {day_5_name} time.

name: Ask for a human
brief: On your first response, ask to speak to a human operator. Do not discuss appointment times first.
